question_title: Will the French government initiate a parliamentary debate or vote under Article 35 of the French Constitution concerning French military involvement in the Iran conflict before May 1, 2026?
summary: France will almost certainly NOT trigger Article 35 constitutional war-powers procedure regarding the Iran conflict before May 1, 2026. Despite deploying warships to the region, France has deliberately framed its involvement as defensive escort/support operations and explicitly declared it is "not a party to the conflict." The March 25 parliamentary debate was held under Article 50-1 (a weaker general declaration procedure), not Article 35. Article 35 has never been invoked in the entire history of the Fifth Republic (65+ years), even during far more direct military engagements. France even co-vetoed the UN Security Council resolution authorizing force to reopen the Strait of Hormuz on April 3, 2026, demonstrating active avoidance of escalation. Only ~24 days remain until the deadline. The sole tail risk is a catastrophic Iranian attack on French forces triggering unprecedented constitutional action.

factors.description: France explicitly states it is NOT a party to the Iran conflict - PM Lecornu and FM Barrot have both said so officially, removing the political predicate for Article 35.
France deliberately framed its military deployment as defensive escort/support, specifically to avoid triggering parliamentary war-powers scrutiny under Article 35.
The March 25 parliamentary debate was held under Article 50-1 (weaker procedure), not Article 35. The government explicitly chose the less binding constitutional path.
Article 35 has NEVER been invoked in the entire Fifth Republic (since 1958) - not for Algeria, Libya, Mali, Syria, or any other French military operation. Zero base rate in 65+ years.
France co-vetoed the UN Security Council resolution authorizing force for the Strait of Hormuz on April 3, 2026, demonstrating active avoidance of military escalation.
Only ~24 days remain until May 1 deadline. A major constitutional shift in this timeframe is extremely unlikely absent unprecedented French military casualties.
Active war with French military assets in proximity to conflict zone; French bases in UAE were attacked by Iran. A catastrophic escalation could theoretically trigger Article 35 demands.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 3% probability (YES)**

The evidence is overwhelmingly against Article 35 being triggered. The key reasons:

- **France says it's not a combatant**: Both PM Lecornu and FM Barrot explicitly stated France is "not a party to the conflict"
- **Deliberate constitutional avoidance**: The government chose Article 50-1 (general debate, no binding vote) over Article 35 for the March 25 parliamentary session
- **Zero historical precedent**: Article 35 has never been invoked in 65+ years of the Fifth Republic, through wars far more directly involving French forces
- **Active de-escalation signals**: France co-vetoed the UN force authorization for the Strait of Hormuz just 4 days ago
- **Short timeline**: Only ~24 days remain, and France's posture is moving away from, not toward, formal war status

The 3% accounts for the small tail risk of a major Iranian attack on French forces that could force an unprecedented constitutional response.

[(Metaculus) Will the French government initiate a parliamentary debate or vote under Article 35 of the French Constitution concerning French military involvement in the Iran conflict before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43061)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the Assemblée nationale or the French government publishes, before May 1, 2026, an official notice or record showing that Article 35 constitutional procedure was triggered in relation to French military involvement in the Iran conflict, using https://www.assemblee-nationale.fr/ or the official government record. Otherwise, this question resolves to no.
fine_print: A general ministerial hearing or statement does not count by itself. The publication must clearly indicate Article 35 procedure tied to the Iran conflict. Candidate Sources: Sénat official site https://www.senat.fr/ ; credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions
